up.method: measured specific consumption rate (residual glucose over time / biomass)

growth_records.cite: Hirose, Yujiro et al. A genome-scale metabolic model of a globally disseminated hyperinvasive M1 strain of Streptococcus pyogenes. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00736-24. [PMC11406949]
growth_records.method: reported (mu from exponential-phase OD600); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rates on glucose, maltose, and dextrin during the exponential phase were 0.229, 0.182, and 0.311 h-1, respectively. The specific consumption rates of glucose, maltose, and dextrin were 19.40, 7.44, and 2.96 mmol g-1 h-1,
growth_records.notes: Measured mu=0.229 h-1 (glucose, exponential phase) and measured specific glucose consumption 19.40 mmol/gDW/h, both reported verbatim in text (Fig 7A/B). doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.229 = 3.027 h (calculated). CDM1 full composition not given inline (ref 4 / Table S6); only carbon source conc 4.5 g/L stated -> medium_composition_stated:false. pH not stated. These experimental rates were used as FBA lower-bound constraints on iYH543. Growth kinetics done in plate reader; general S. pyogenes cultivation stated as 37C, 5% CO2, THY.

up.method: measured specific consumption rate (residual maltose over time / biomass)

growth_records.cite: Hirose, Yujiro et al. A genome-scale metabolic model of a globally disseminated hyperinvasive M1 strain of Streptococcus pyogenes. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00736-24. [PMC11406949]
growth_records.method: reported (mu from exponential-phase OD600); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rates on glucose, maltose, and dextrin during the exponential phase were 0.229, 0.182, and 0.311 h-1, respectively. The specific consumption rates of glucose, maltose, and dextrin were 19.40, 7.44, and 2.96 mmol g-1 h-1,
growth_records.notes: Measured mu=0.182 h-1 (maltose) and measured specific maltose consumption 7.44 mmol/gDW/h, reported verbatim (Fig 7A/B). doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.182 = 3.808 h (calculated). Maltose = disaccharide of D-glucose. CDM1 composition not inline; carbon conc 4.5 g/L stated -> medium_composition_stated:false. pH not stated.

up.method: measured specific consumption rate (residual dextrin over time / biomass); dextrin modeled as gamma-cyclodextrin (8 linked D-glucose)

growth_records.cite: Hirose, Yujiro et al. A genome-scale metabolic model of a globally disseminated hyperinvasive M1 strain of Streptococcus pyogenes. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00736-24. [PMC11406949]
growth_records.method: reported (mu from exponential-phase OD600); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rates on glucose, maltose, and dextrin during the exponential phase were 0.229, 0.182, and 0.311 h-1, respectively. The specific consumption rates of glucose, maltose, and dextrin were 19.40, 7.44, and 2.96 mmol g-1 h-1,
growth_records.notes: Measured mu=0.311 h-1 (dextrin, highest of the three) and measured specific dextrin consumption 2.96 mmol/gDW/h, reported verbatim (Fig 7A/B). doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.311 = 2.229 h (calculated). Dextrin (starch) consumed/modeled as gamma-cyclodextrin, eight alpha-1,4-linked D-glucose subunits. CDM1 composition not inline; carbon conc 4.5 g/L stated -> medium_composition_stated:false. pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: Human decidua tissue (ex vivo infection model)
growth_records.cite: Lambert, Clara et al. The double-edged role of FASII regulator FabT in Streptococcus pyogenes infection. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-52637-3. [PMC11452403]
growth_records.snippet: the WT strain grew with a doubling time of roughly 200 min whereas the mFabT strain did not grow
growth_records.notes: Growth rate calculated from reported approximate doubling time: td ~200 min = 3.33 h; mu = ln2/td = 0.693/3.33 = 0.208 /h. Value is explicitly 'roughly 200 min', so approximate. Growth is on human decidua tissue (host infection model), not a defined prokaryote medium, so not directly usable as a GEM uptake/secretion constraint. Companion condition: the mFabT (FabT H105Y) mutant did NOT grow on decidua (no numeric rate). Metabolomic 'consumption' of hexoses and amino acids (Asn, Ile, Lys, Ser) by mFabT vs WT was reported only as relative/differential abundances in conditioned supernatants (Fig 5, Suppl Table 5), with no absolute quantitative uptake rates and no biomass normalization, so no uptake_rate records could be derived. In standard THY / THY-Tween lab medium WT and mFabT grow similarly but no numeric doubling time/mu is given.

phenotypes.base_medium: Phenotype-microarray / substrate-utilisation assay (defined minimal + sole substrate)
phenotypes.citation: Hirose et al. (2024) [PMC11406949]

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 14762 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/14762

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
